interest: The interest behind this argument is that of general-school teachers, disadvantaged and Roma pupils/families, and long-horizon planners (employers, policy researchers) who need the compensatory resources to actually persist rather than evaporate at the first budget cycle.
value: The value in tension is durability/reliability of equity provision versus fiscal-political flexibility, with an underlying equity-vs-status-quo-continuity conflict since fragile compensation leaves early sorting intact but unremedied.
fear: The real fear is that teachers will build remedial/diagnostic capacity and families will depend on compensatory resources, only to have the funding cut in a consolidation cycle — leaving permanent sorting, workload, and dependency but no resources.
affected: families of children in general schools
employers relying on the talent pipeline
future governments inheriting the entrenched system
assumption: The unstated assumption is that without constitutional or supermajority ring-fencing, ordinary legislative budget commitments to targeted funding will predictably be reversed under fiscal pressure.
empirical_uncertainty: The factual core — that targeted-funding line items are historically among the first cut in fiscal consolidation — is presented as a historical pattern in the scenario and treated as a serious rather than notional risk, but whether a specific Hungarian ring-fencing mechanism would hold is unknown and contested.
